findings.title: Add negative tests for new URMA failover gflag validators
findings.impact: The PR adds validation that must reject non-finite or out-of-range success-rate ratios and a zero min-sample count, but the added UT/ST coverage only assigns valid values directly or writes valid config files. A regression that accepts `-urma_failover_success_rate_ratio=nan`, `1.1`, or `-urma_failover_min_sample_count=0` would leave an unsafe failover policy accepted by startup or hot config parsing without any test failing.
findings.fix: Add focused flag tests, for example in `tests/ut/common/flags/flags_test.cpp`, that call `SetCommandLineOption` or `ParseCommandLineFlags` for `urma_failover_success_rate_ratio` values below 0, above 1, and non-finite inputs, plus `urma_failover_min_sample_count=0`, asserting rejection and preservation of the previous/default value. Keep the existing tracker/ST tests for valid dynamic-update behavior.
